title: Directors Cuts of 25 26 on Genesis Reborn
display_title: Directors Cuts of 25 26 on Genesis Reborn
wikitext:
[]: "Director's Cuts" of 25-26 on Genesis Reborn             Quote Post by Unit007 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:38 am I tried searching but found nothing so sorry if this is a repeat. so I thought there were only directors cut episodes of 21 through 24 that were scenes from death spliced in that can be found on platinum releases and genesis reborn. So my question is what is on this directors cut edition of episodes 25 and 26 called genesis resurrection? On the back of the dvd it says it has unaired material and what not. Does that mean there is stuff from EoE on there? Thinking about buying it but confused to what would be on it. Any help on this matter would be greatly appreciated. My sanity will thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:45 am ADV claimed that there were 6 for that release, which I think was done to justify splitting it unnecessarily onto two discs for more sales. In actuality, what is on the Genesis Reborn DVD is the "On Air" and "Video" versions of episodes 25 and 26. You probably won't be able to tell the difference between the two. There are no new scenes in the "director's cut" of 25-26 like in episodes 21'-24'. If you buy Resurrection and Genesis Reborn, you will probably be disappointed, unless you're an obsessive collector like me.

[]:             Quote Post by Unit007 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 10:52 am So basically the back of the dvd is just lying to me then. Hmmm.... ADV really likes to just milk it I guess huh? Thanks for the info man. Or should I say monkey;)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 14, 2011 11:08 am I'm not sure about lying, but they weren't entirely honest with their releases and marketing of those releases: ADV Films today announced formally that the firm will release the Director’s Cut of the final six episodes of the enormously influential anime series “Neon Genesis Evangelion.” These Director’s Cut episodes include additional footage, never before released in the U.S. ADV Films expects to release the Director’s Cut in two three-episode DVD volumes; each volume will include both the extended Director’s Cut versions and the original versions of the episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 12:49 pm Well, Genesis Reborn has episodes 24-26 and 24 has new scenes, so they're probably using that to make it sound like all three are different.

[]:             Quote Post by Unit007 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 1:29 pm Well I can see how they aren't lying about both of them containing director's cut episodes together but the actual back of the dvd itself in the description it says never before seen footage and that 25 and 26 are director's cut.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 14, 2011 2:19 pm Unit007 wrote: ...and that 25 and 26 are director's cut.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 14, 2011 2:36 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Indeed it does, which is NOT true. No such thing exists.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:02 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Didn't the "director's cut" of episode 25 have a different preview than OA? But other then that, I don't remember there being any new footage in the actual episode. And there's absolutely nothing new in episode 26.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:34 pm If I recall correctly ( which in this case may not be so ) the original ADV VHS/DVD releases that contained Episodes 25-26 used unaltered on-air masters from the Japanese TV broadcast. Since the US VHS releases (both sub and dub) containing said episodes was put out BEFORE the Japanese VHS/LD releases (which had slight alterations) of 25-26, it would make sense. My theory is that the ADV "Director's Cut" DVDs called the Japanese home video transfers of 25-26 "Director's Cuts" since they were slightly updated as compared to what they used previously...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:42 pm svenge wrote: If I recall correctly ( which in this case may not be so ) the original ADV VHS/DVD releases that contained Episodes 25-26 used unaltered on-air masters from the Japanese TV broadcast. Since the US VHS releases (both sub and dub) containing said episodes was put out BEFORE the Japanese VHS/LD releases (which had slight alterations) of 25-26, it would make sense. My theory is that the ADV "Director's Cut" DVDs called the Japanese home video transfers of 25-26 "Director's Cuts" since they were slightly updated as compared to what they used previously...

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:08 pm If ADV had the rights to EoE all this time, the use of the term "Director's cuts of 25 and 26" might have been true to their word. ...ok, so Anno approved 25 and 26 regardless on how everyone views it, but if fans expect from ADV new footage for 25 and 26... you know, ADV really should have gotten the rights for EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:25 pm tomrule123 wrote: ADV really should have gotten the rights for EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Fri Apr 15, 2011 12:08 am There's no added footage technicly, the preview for 25 was replaced with an cut of animatics for 25'. At least on the Platinum Holiday version, though.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 15, 2011 12:37 pm Alastor wrote: There's no added footage technicly, the preview for 25 was replaced with an cut of animatics for 25'. At least on the Platinum Holiday version, though.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Fri Apr 15, 2011 1:04 pm svenge wrote: If I recall correctly ( which in this case may not be so ) the original ADV VHS/DVD releases that contained Episodes 25-26 used unaltered on-air masters from the Japanese TV broadcast. Since the US VHS releases (both sub and dub) containing said episodes was put out BEFORE the Japanese VHS/LD releases (which had slight alterations) of 25-26, it would make sense. My theory is that the ADV "Director's Cut" DVDs called the Japanese home video transfers of 25-26 "Director's Cuts" since they were slightly updated as compared to what they used previously...

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 15, 2011 3:06 pm LordEvan88 wrote: I watched these last night, and I also noticed they used the redubbing actors for 25,26 (I.E. John Swayse as Gendo). Just an interesting observation, it was probably just done for consistency since they do indeed have no new content. Also the standard platinum DVDs don't have the Air preview, which makes me sad. But there is always Youtube.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Fri Apr 15, 2011 8:00 pm Holiday has 21'-24' though, so it's possibly that.
